steps.title: Identify the relationship between risk perception and purchase likelihood
steps.content: The key signal is the relationship Tarabieh established: higher perceived environmental risk leads to lower purchase likelihood among environmentally conscious consumers. The Australian study found that middle-aged adults have the lowest perceived environmental risk among all demographic groups.

steps.title: Apply the relationship to identify the prediction
steps.content:
Since middle-aged adults have the lowest perceived environmental risk, they should be most likely to purchase the TV under Tarabieh's framework where lower risk perception predicts higher purchase likelihood.

The other choices either attribute the decision to unmeasured factors (A), reference products not discussed (C), or contradict the study's logical result (D).

This matches the correct answer — B is correct.
